channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Microsoft Defender for Cloud Customer Newsletter
item.description: What's new in Defender for Cloud? Kubernetes gated deployment is now generally available for AKS automatic clusters. Use help to deploy the Defender for Containers sensor to use this feature. More information can be found here. Grouped recommendations are converted into individual ones to list each...
item.dc:creator: Yura_Lee

item.title: Model Upgrade and Migration Strategy for Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Model upgrades in Microsoft Foundry are not a one‑time platform operation, they are an ongoing application lifecycle discipline. Microsoft documentation makes this explicit: models evolve continuously, older versions are eventually retired, and customers are expected to evaluate, migrate, and...
item.dc:creator: Dr_Ravi_Nagvekar

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity in organization reports
item.description: Following our enterprise-level, user-level, and organization-level CLI metrics releases, we’re completing coverage with per-user CLI breakdowns in organization reports. Organization admins can now see which individual users are active on… The post Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates
item.description: This month, GitHub Actions adds entrypoint and command overrides for service containers and new security features including OIDC custom properties and VNET failover. Customizing entrypoints for service containers Many GitHub… The post GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Explore union types in C# 15
item.description: C# 15 introduces union types — declare a closed set of case types with implicit conversions and exhaustive pattern matching. Try unions in preview today and see the broader exhaustiveness roadmap. The post Explore union types in C# 15 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Bill Wagner

item.title: Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain)
item.description: Aspire 13.2 ships aspire docs, a CLI for browsing, searching, and reading official aspire.dev docs from your terminal, with the same source of truth available to AI skills and automation. The post Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain) appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Pine

item.title: Retirement: Python support for Azure App Service and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027
item.description: Starting March 31, 2027, Python applications hosted on Azure App Service on Windows and Azure Functions on Windows will no longer run. After this date: Your application configuration and content will remain intact Your apps will stop running
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description: AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will enter retirement in March 2027. Customers leveraging AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) should transition to Standard endpoints for all new and existing blob storage account deployments b
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface
item.description: Generative AI is upgrading cyberattacks, from 450% higher phishing click‑through rates to industrialized MFA bypass. The post Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Sherrod DeGrippo

item.title: How Do We Know AI Isn’t Lying? The Art of Evaluating LLMs in RAG Systems
item.description: 🔍 1. Why Evaluating LLM Responses is Hard In classical programming, correctness is binary. InputExpectedResult2 + 24✔ Correct2 + 25✘ Wrong Software is deterministic — same input → same output. LLMs are probabilistic. They generate one of many valid word combinations, like forming sentences from...
item.dc:creator: ditisaxena

item.title: Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy tradecraft in Linux hosting environments
item.description: Cookie-gated PHP webshells use obfuscation, php-fpm execution, and cron-based persistence to evade detection in Linux hosting environments. This post examines how this tradecraft conceals execution behind specially crafted HTTP cookies. The post Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Copilot Notebooks: Enhancements to support creation, collaboration and learning
item.description: Copilot Notebooks are AI-powered workspaces for a project or topic built on your reference materials—bringing together your work context behind a project in one place for you, your team members, and Copilot to collaborate on. Whether you need a mind map to better understand a complex concept or a...
item.dc:creator: ezrapark

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update
item.description: March 2026 brought a major step forward for GitHub Copilot extensibility in Visual Studio, with custom agents, agent skills, and new tools that make the agent smarter and more capable.… The post GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Introducing MAI-Transcribe-1, MAI-Voice-1, and MAI-Image-2 in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: Another Step Towards a Complete AI Platform Since inception, our goal with Microsoft Foundry has been to deliver the most complete AI and app agent factory; giving developers access to the latest frontier models, tools, infrastructure, security, and reliability to confidently build and scale their...
item.dc:creator: Naomi Moneypenny

item.title: The Security tab is now Security & quality
item.description: The top-level Security tab across repositories, organizations, and enterprises has been renamed to Security & quality on github.com. This change restructures the navigation to colocate code quality findings alongside security… The post The Security tab is now Security & quality appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available
item.description: Finding the right issue just got easier. First introduced in public preview in January and expanded to the Issues dashboard in February, improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally… The post Improved search for GitHub Issues is now generally available appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot organization custom instructions are generally available
item.description: Organization custom instructions for GitHub Copilot, first introduced in April 2025, are now generally available. With organization custom instructions, Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise organization administrators can set default instructions… The post Copilot organization custom...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Unlocking AI-Ready Unstructured Data at Scale with Komprise and Azure
item.description: Why Move Unstructured Data to Azure On-premises storage environments are often over-provisioned to accommodate future growth, driving costs and operational complexity. Azure’s cloud-based storage platform enables organizations to right-size their environments through elastic scaling and Microsoft’s...
item.dc:creator: dmalbrough

item.title: VS Code Custom Agents: AI-Powered Terraform Security Scanning in the IDE
item.description: GitHub Copilot is already a powerful coding assistant, but out of the box it knows nothing specific about your project's conventions, security requirements, or operational processes. Custom agents change that. They let you define specialized AI assistants that live inside your repository, carry...
item.dc:creator: SundarBalajiA

item.title: Build a Fully Offline AI App with Foundry Local and CAG
item.description: A hands-on guide to building an on-device AI support agent using Context-Augmented Generation, JavaScript, and Foundry Local. You have probably heard the AI pitch: "just call our API." But what happens when your application needs to work without an internet connection? Perhaps your users are field...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: Understanding the Total Cost of Ownership
item.description: Whether you're just beginning your journey in Azure or are already managing workloads in the cloud, it's essential to ground your strategy in proven guidance. The Microsoft Cloud Adoption Framework for Azure offers a comprehensive set of best practices, documentation, and tools to help you align...
item.dc:creator: MariaJoseF

item.title: Orchestrating Intrusion Detection and Prevention Signature overrides in Azure Firewall Premium
item.description: Introduction: Azure Firewall Premium provides strong protection with a built-in Intrusion Detection and Prevention System (IDPS). It inspects inbound, outbound, and east-west traffic against Microsoft’s continuously updated signature set and can block threats before they reach your workloads. IDPS...
item.dc:creator: saikishor

item.title: Announcing Private Preview: Deploy Ansible Playbooks using Azure Policy via Machine Configuration
item.description: Azure Arc is on a mission to unify security, compliance, and management for Windows and Linux machines—anywhere. By extending Azure’s control plane beyond the cloud, Azure Arc enables organizations to unify governance, compliance, security and management of servers across on‑premises, edge, and...
item.dc:creator: alinetran

item.title: Announcing AWS with Azure SRE Agent: Cross-Cloud Investigation using the brand new AWS DevOps Agent
item.description: Overview Connect Azure SRE Agent to AWS services using the official AWS MCP server. Query AWS documentation, execute any of the 15,000+ AWS APIs, run operational workflows, and kick off incident investigations through AWS DevOps Agent, which is now generally available. The AWS MCP server connects...
item.dc:creator: dbandaru

item.title: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description: Service Fabric will end support for all clusters running on Windows Server 2022 on March 31, 2027. Customers must upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by this date to remain in a supported state. This change aligns Service Fabric’s Windows OS image support mode
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Mitigating the Axios npm supply chain compromise
item.description: On March 31, 2026, the popular HTTP client Axios experienced a supply chain attack, causing two newly published npm packages for version updates to download from command and control (C2) that Microsoft Threat Intelligence has attributed to the North Korean state actor Sapphire Sleet. Although the...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description: Service Fabric will end support for all clusters running on Windows Server 2019 on March 31, 2027. Customers must upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by this date to remain in a supported state. This change aligns Service Fabric’s Windows OS image support mode
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues is now Generally Available
item.description: We’re excited to announce that user delegation (UD) SAS is now generally available for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues in all regions. User delegation SAS is already available for Azure Blobs, and we are now extending support to Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues. This will...
item.dc:creator: ellievail

item.title: Announcing Microsoft Azure Network Adapter (MANA) support for Existing VM SKUs
item.description: As a leader in cloud infrastructure, Microsoft ensures that Azure’s IaaS customers always have access to the latest hardware. Our goal is to consistently deliver technology to support business critical workloads with world class efficiency, reliability, and security. Customers benefit from...
item.dc:creator: ali_sheriff

item.title: A third-party connector integrating Claude with Microsoft Sentinel is now available
item.description: Security teams are increasingly exploring how AI assistants can support them in investigating incidents, asking questions, and exploring their data. At the same time, protecting that data, and controlling how it’s accessed, remains critical. Today, we’re sharing how Sentinel can securely support a...
item.dc:creator: mcasgrain

item.title: Securing the open source supply chain across GitHub
item.description: Recent attacks on open source focus on exfiltrating secrets; here are the prevention steps you can take today, plus a look at the security capabilities GitHub is working on. The post Securing the open source supply chain across GitHub appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Steindler

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Unlock Client-Side Configuration at Scale with Azure App Configuration and Azure Front Door
item.description: You can now deliver dynamic configuration directly to client-side applications—securely and at CDN scale—thanks to Azure App Configuration’s integration with Azure Front Door. This delivers new flexibility for modern apps and unlocks a critical capability
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: DPDK 25.11 Performance on Azure for High-Speed Packet Workloads
item.description: At Microsoft Azure, performance is treated as an ongoing discipline grounded in careful engineering and real-world validation. As cloud workloads grow in scale and variety, customers depend on consistent, high-throughput networking. Technologies such as the Data Plane Development Kit (DPDK) play a...
item.dc:creator: KashanK

item.title: RSAC 2026: New Microsoft Sentinel Connectors Announcement
item.description: Microsoft Sentinel helps organizations detect, investigate, and respond to security threats across increasingly complex environments. With the rollout of the Microsoft Sentinel data lake in the fall, and the App Assure-backed Sentinel promise that supports it, customers now have access to...
item.dc:creator: JesseKopavi

item.title: Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent
item.description: Copilot cloud agent (formerly known as Copilot coding agent) is no longer limited to pull-request workflows, unlocking a broader range of ways to put Copilot to work. More control over… The post Research, plan, and code with Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box enhances compliance with automatic Secure Erasure Certificates
item.description: Azure Data Box now automatically generates a downloadable Secure Erasure Certificate for every completed order, providing verification that all data on the device has been securely erased in accordance with NIST 800-88 Revision 2 standards. The certific
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box now supports Azure Files Provisioned v2
item.description: Azure Data Box now supports ingestion of data into Azure Files Provisioned v2 storage accounts. Azure Files Provisioned v2 is a billing model where customers provision and pay for specific capacity, IOPS, and throughput values; giving greater flexibility
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In development] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files storage with cool access enhancement
item.description: QoS update with cool access enabled on Premium and Ultra service levels improves how Azure NetApp Files balances performance and cost for mixed hot and cool workloads. Throughput automatically adjusts as data moves to cool storage, maintaining hot‑tier pe
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Select Azure AI Language Features
item.description: On March 20, 2027, Microsoft will retire the following eight Azure AI Language features: Key Phrase ExtractionSentiment Analysis and Opinion MiningCustom Text ClassificationConversational Language Understanding (CLU)Custom Question Answering (CQA)Orchestr
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Improving the Markdown Editor for Work Items
item.description: We introduced the Markdown editor in July 2025 to bring Markdown support to large text fields in work items. Since then, we’ve received valuable customer feedback highlighting challenges with the editing experience, particularly when switching in and out of edit mode. Many users found the current...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise with data residency
item.description: GitHub Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency. You can create secure, configurable, instantly available cloud development environments while maintaining strict compliance with your data… The post Codespaces is now generally available for GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.114
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.114 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection
item.description: GPT-5.4 mini is now generally available to Copilot Student plan via Copilot auto model selection. This model is part of Auto in GitHub Copilot Chat on Visual Studio Code, Visual… The post GPT-5.4 mini is now available in Copilot Student auto model selection appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Mobile: Stay in flow with a refreshed Copilot tab and native session logs
item.description: GitHub Mobile now makes it easier to manage agentic workflows on the go with a refreshed Copilot tab, native session logs, and more in-app controls for agent sessions. On Android,… The post GitHub Mobile: Stay in flow with a refreshed Copilot tab and native session logs appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The Polyglot tax – Part 3
item.description: Vectors, Analytics, and the End of ETL Part 3 of 4 – The Multimodal Database Series In Part 1: The Polyglot Tax we laid out the fraud detection scenario: a transaction comes in, and before you approve or deny it you need five checks. Order history (relational). Device fingerprint (JSON)....
item.dc:creator: Aditya Badramraju

item.title: Proactive Reliability Series — Article 1: Fault Types in Azure
item.description: Welcome to the Proactive Reliability Series — a collection of articles dedicated to raising awareness about the importance of designing, implementing, and operating reliable solutions in Azure. Each article will focus on a specific area of reliability engineering: from identifying critical flows...
item.dc:creator: Zoran Jovanovic

item.title: Migrating Azure SQL Database Across Tenants Using Subscription Transfer
item.description: Moving an Azure subscription between Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) tenants is a common requirement during: Mergers and acquisitions Organizational restructuring Enterprise Agreement (EA) enrollment realignment Lighthouse or multi‑tenant operating model changes However, when...
item.dc:creator: princy_rajpoot

item.title: Run multiple agents at once with /fleet in Copilot CLI
item.description: /fleet lets Copilot CLI dispatch multiple agents in parallel. Learn how to write prompts that split work across files, declare dependencies, and avoid common pitfalls. The post Run multiple agents at once with /fleet in Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Matt Nigh

item.title: 🔥Agentic AI Email Bot on AWS Bedrock AgentCore: Full Architecture and O365 Integration (1/2)🔥
item.description: aka, yes your inbox can have a robot in it now
item.dc:creator: Alex Moore

item.title: Bookmark Studio: evolving bookmarks in Visual Studio
item.description: Bookmarks in Visual Studio have always been a simple, reliable feature. Many developers use them regularly, and over the years we’ve heard consistent feedback from those users. Bookmarks were useful, but there were a few core gaps that kept them from being as effective and relevant as they could...
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen
